github.name: Cognitive-Core-Skills
github.description: A universal, industry-neutral taxonomy of cognitive core skills (perception, memory, reasoning, planning, action, verification, learning, governance) for LLMs, SLMs, AI agents, and world models — with schemas, 159 skill cards, benchmarks, and CI.

github.description: Train a tiny Transformer from scratch on your iMessage history, entirely on your Mac.

github.description: CPU-first tensor/autograd runtime and LLM inference engine in pure Zig — eager execution, GGUF in and out, LoRA training on CPU, benchmarked pairwise against llama.cpp.

trends: Today's tech trends reveal a strong push towards highly capable, proactive AI agents that can not only reason complexly but also interact with and control the physical world. There's significant interest in optimizing AI for efficiency and local deployment, enabling personalized models and privacy-preserving applications, moving beyond cloud-only solutions. Concurrently, AI's role in automating digital tasks, orchestrating content creation across platforms, and enhancing scientific discovery through advanced reasoning and knowledge lineage is rapidly expanding. Alongside these technical advancements, the developer community is keenly focused on personal growth, combatting burnout, fostering deep understanding of systems (rather than superficial knowledge), and leveraging continuous learning for career longevity and well-being.

ideas.title: Proactive Home Intelligence Agent
ideas.description: Develop an AI agent that integrates with smart home platforms (e.g., Home Assistant, Apple HomeKit via bridge). Unlike standard voice assistants, this agent uses local sensor data (presence, temperature, light, calendar events) and a small, personalized LLM to learn user routines and proactively manage home settings. For example, it could adjust lighting based on natural light levels and user activity patterns, or pre-heat water based on morning calendar events, without explicit commands. It should offer explanations for its actions and allow user feedback to refine its model, promoting a more intuitive and responsive smart home experience.
tech_stack: Home Assistant API / HomeKit API (via bridge)
Locally runnable LLM (e.g., Llama.cpp via `llama-cpp-python` or Ollama)
MQTT / REST for sensor data ingestion
Flask/FastAPI for a simple web UI/API
steps: Set up a local LLM environment using `llama-cpp-python` or Ollama for inference.
Integrate with a smart home platform's API (e.g., Home Assistant REST API) to read sensor data and send commands.
Develop an agent logic core in Python that ingests sensor data and calendar events.
Use the local LLM to interpret context, learn routines, and suggest/generate proactive actions.
Implement a feedback mechanism (e.g., via a simple web interface or notification) for users to approve/deny agent actions, refining the agent's behavior.
Add a module to provide concise explanations for the agent's decisions and actions.

ideas.title: OmniCampaign AI Agent Fleet Commander
ideas.description: Build a system that acts as an orchestrator for a 'fleet' of specialized AI agents to execute multi-platform content campaigns. The user provides a high-level campaign goal (e.g., 'Launch a 3-day awareness campaign for our new product X on all social media'). The main orchestrating agent, drawing inspiration from systems like 'a-fable-of-codexes', breaks down the goal, assigns sub-tasks to specialized worker agents (e.g., a text generation agent, an image generation agent, a video snippet agent), manages the content creation process (text, image, short video clips), and schedules posts across various social media platforms (e.g., Facebook, X, Threads) using a unified interface, similar to 'OmniPost-AI'.
tech_stack: Python (with LangChain or similar agent framework)
OpenAI API / Claude API / Gemini API (for different agent types)
Image generation APIs (e.g., DALL-E, Stability AI)
Video generation APIs (e.g., RunwayML, Pika Labs if available, or simple `ffmpeg` orchestration)
Social media APIs (Facebook Graph API, X API, Threads API)
Message queue (e.g., RabbitMQ, Celery) for task management
Web framework (e.g., Django, Next.js) for user interface
ideas.source_inspiration: GitHub Repositories: 'a-fable-of-codexes', 'OmniPost-AI'; Hacker News: 'AI-generated videos to maximally drive a target brain region'; arXiv Papers: 'OpenCoF'
steps: Design the core orchestrator agent logic using an agent framework like LangChain to manage campaign goals and sub-tasks.
Integrate various generative AI APIs for text (LLMs), images, and potentially short video clips.
Develop specialized 'worker' agents (e.g., `TextGenerationAgent`, `ImageGenerationAgent`, `VideoSnippetAgent`, `SocialMediaPostingAgent`).
Implement task decomposition, delegation, and result aggregation mechanisms between the orchestrator and worker agents.
Integrate social media publishing APIs for the target platforms (Facebook, X, Threads).
Create a user interface where users can define campaign objectives, monitor content generation, and approve/schedule posts.
Implement error handling and retry mechanisms for API calls and agent failures.

ideas.title: DocPilot: Context-Aware Developer Learning Agent
ideas.description: Create an IDE extension or desktop application that acts as a personalized learning companion and documentation navigator. It ingests a developer's local codebase, project-specific documentation, and optionally external library documentation. When the developer is working on a specific code file, the agent uses a locally runnable LLM to provide context-aware summaries of relevant documentation, explain complex system parts, or generate tailored code examples based on the developer's *current project context*. It also analyzes implicit knowledge gaps (e.g., based on code patterns or frequent lookup queries) and suggests targeted 'deep dives' or '100-days-of-AI'-style learning modules from general resources, encouraging a deeper understanding of systems and preventing superficial knowledge.
tech_stack: Locally runnable LLM (e.g., Ollama or custom `llama.cpp` integration)
IDE extension framework (e.g., VS Code Extension API)
Vector database (e.g., FAISS, ChromaDB) for local document embeddings
ideas.source_inspiration: GitHub Repositories: '100-days-of-ai', 'texts-to-transformer', 'fucina'; Dev.to Articles: 'you stopped reading the docs. now you don't understand the systems.', 'Your Career Matters. So Does the Person Building It.'
steps: Set up a local LLM server (e.g., Ollama) or integrate `llama.cpp` directly for contextual understanding.
Develop parsers to ingest local codebase (AST, Tree-sitter) and project/external documentation (Markdown, RST).
Implement document chunking and embedding generation for a local vector store to enable semantic search.
Build an IDE extension (e.g., for VS Code) to detect the current file, cursor position, and selected code context.
Develop agent logic to query the LLM and vector store based on the developer's active context, providing summaries, explanations, or code examples.
Implement a 'knowledge gap identifier' that suggests relevant learning resources or 'deep dives' based on contextual analysis.
Design a user-friendly interface within the IDE extension for interaction, displaying insights, and managing learning paths.

quick_actions: Dedicate 30 minutes daily to deep documentation review: Instead of quick searches, pick a core technology or framework you use and read a section of its official documentation or specification. Focus on understanding underlying principles.

Implement a small AI agent for a personal micro-automation task: Identify one simple, repetitive digital task (e.g., categorizing screenshots, generating meeting summaries, drafting email replies) and build a tiny, script-based AI agent using a local LLM (like Ollama) to automate or semi-automate it.

Schedule 15 minutes weekly for 'AI Trend Synthesis' reading: Set aside dedicated time each week to review high-level AI/ML news, summaries, or analyses (like this one!) to stay abreast of agentic AI advancements without getting bogged down in every technical detail.

Practice mindful disengagement from tech for 30 minutes before bed: Actively put away all devices and engage in a non-screen activity (e.g., reading a physical book, light stretching, journaling). This habit supports mental well-being and helps prevent burnout, fostering sustainable productivity.
